title: 100x BG-11 media
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b58q5","text":"This protocol describes how to prepare a 100x BG-11 +N stock.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"epq3e","text":"1x BG-11 media could be created from the stock and supplemental stocks and trace metals could be added afterwards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7mh4t","text":"This protocol is based on Anne Behle M.Sc. Reciepe for standard BG-11 media protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":43,"length":32}],"data":[]},{"key":"7ijti","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"736m5","text":"Recipes for standard and alternative BG11 for culturing freshwater cyanobacteria, such as Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803, as described.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":90,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d30lg","text":"Media is usually not suitable for marine cyanobacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"80BE51D4B71A438E9A5A0BB3A0A8FCEE","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/recipe-for-standard-bg-11-media-gtmbwk6"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"83t0p","text":" Always work under sterile conditions when handling sterile media or stocks. Work under the clean bench.\n ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add CaCl 2 2H 2 O (3.6 g · L -1 ). We did not have hydrated CaCl 2 . Therefore, we used 2.718 g CaCl 2 (powder) for 1L of the stock.
Add MgSO 4 · 7 H 2 O (7.49 g · L -1 ). We do not have hydrated MgSO 4 . Therefore, we used 3.659 g MgSO 4 (powder) for 1L of the stock.

Add 0.25 M Na 2 -EDTA, pH 8.0 (0.56 ml · L -1 ). We did not have a 0.25 M Na 2 -EDTA stock. Therefore, we dissolved 2.32 g Na 2 -EDTA (powder) and filled the bottle up with MilliQ (nuclease-free) water up to 25 mL. Afterwards, the pH was adjusted with diluted NaOH to pH = 8.0.
The bottle was filled with MilliQ (nuclease-free) water up to 1L and mixed a big stir bar until the powder was completely dissolved and the solution was clear.
Afterwards, the stir bar was removed and the media was directly autoclaved at 120 °C for 30 Minutes.
creator: Miriam D
